description:
A client has provided you with the raw *text* output of an `openssl x509 -text` command that was run against one of their legacy services.  
Your job, acting as a penetration-tester, is to extract the most important certificate-lifetime data, decide whether the certificate is self-signed and/or expired, and then record your findings in a **single** CSV log file so that the client’s automated compliance pipeline can ingest it.

Files/directories already present
1. /home/user/targets/old_server_certificate.txt  
   – The file holds the verbatim human-readable certificate dump that the client sent you (see “exact contents” in the truth section).  
2. /home/user/audit/      (empty directory with write permission for the current user)

What you must do
1. Read /home/user/targets/old_server_certificate.txt and extract **exactly** the following fields (do not include leading or trailing spaces):  
   • Subject CN (everything after `Subject:`)  
   • Issuer CN (everything after `Issuer:`)  
   • Not Before date/time (everything after `Not Before:`)  
   • Not After  date/time (everything after `Not After :`)  

2. Convert the two date/time values from the “Apr 1 00:00:00 2022 GMT” style to strict ISO-8601 UTC format **YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ**  
   Example:  
     Apr  1 00:00:00 2022 GMT  →  2022-04-01T00:00:00Z  

3. Decide the following booleans:  
   • expired – TRUE if the *current* system date is later than the converted **Not After** value, otherwise FALSE.  
   • self_signed – TRUE if Subject CN and Issuer CN are identical, otherwise FALSE.

4. Create the log file /home/user/audit/cert_scan.log with **exactly** these two lines (including the header) and **no additional whitespace**:  
   target,subject,issuer,notBefore,notAfter,expired,self_signed  
   old_server,<Subject CN>,<Issuer CN>,<ISO_NotBefore>,<ISO_NotAfter>,<TRUE/FALSE>,<TRUE/FALSE>  

   For this dataset the correct finished line (after your substitutions) is expected to be:  
   old_server,CN=Old Vulnerable Server,CN=Old Vulnerable Server,2022-04-01T00:00:00Z,2023-04-01T00:00:00Z,TRUE,TRUE

   Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline.

5. Display the contents of /home/user/audit/cert_scan.log to the console (so the client sees the summary immediately).

Verification notes for you
• The CSV must use commas only (**,**) as separators – no tabs or extra spaces.  
• Case matters: use TRUE / FALSE in all-caps.  
• Date strings must contain the literal **T** between the date and time and end with **Z**.

The entire workflow can easily be completed in roughly 5-10 standard shell commands (mkdir/cat/grep/awk/printf or similar). No root privileges are required.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts)

1. Directory structure
   /home/user/
     ├── targets/
     │     └── old_server_certificate.txt
     └── audit/          (empty, perms 0755 owned by user)

2. Exact contents of /home/user/targets/old_server_certificate.txt  (including leading spaces):
Certificate:
    Data:
        Version: 3 (0x2)
        Serial Number:
            04:00:00:00:00:01 (268435457)
    Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption
        Issuer: CN=Old Vulnerable Server
        Validity
            Not Before: Apr  1 00:00:00 2022 GMT
            Not After : Apr  1 00:00:00 2023 GMT
        Subject: CN=Old Vulnerable Server
        Subject Public Key Info:
            Public Key Algorithm: rsaEncryption
                Public-Key: (2048 bit)
                Modulus:
                    00:c6:33:24:2a:4c:5e:3b:c5:70:b0...
                Exponent: 65537 (0x10001)
        X509v3 extensions:
            X509v3 Basic Constraints:
                CA:TRUE
            X509v3 Key Usage: critical
                Digital Signature, Key Cert Sign, CRL Sign
    Signature Algorithm: sha256WithRSAEncryption
         53:71:a1:9a:ce:9b:9a:8c:44:6b:c4:49:73:41:69...
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

(Expected) final state after successful completion

1. A new file /home/user/audit/cert_scan.log exists with mode 0644 containing exactly:
target,subject,issuer,notBefore,notAfter,expired,self_signed
old_server,CN=Old Vulnerable Server,CN=Old Vulnerable Server,2022-04-01T00:00:00Z,2023-04-01T00:00:00Z,TRUE,TRUE

(ends with a single newline)

The automated test will:
• Confirm that /home/user/audit/cert_scan.log exists and matches the above two-line exact content.  
• Reject files that have extra spaces, missing header, wrong TRUE/FALSE capitalisation, or missing trailing newline.
name: task_000000_65c500e5
